Ω : Type u_2

G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V
p q : ↑unitInterval
P : Measure Ω

hY : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m H q P
⊢ AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P

1. `(G, H)` is a.e. measurable.

But how to get that `(G, H)` is a.e. measurable? 

Lean's `MeasureTheory` has `AEMeasurable.prod`, which says that if `f` and `g` are a.e. measurable, then `(f, g)` is a.e. measurable.

Here is an approach:

1. For any `u v : V`, the map `(G, H) ↦ (G ⊓ H).adj u v = G.adj u v ∧ H.adj u v` is measurable because `G ↦ G.adj u v` is measurable and `H ↦ H.adj u v` is measurable (since `adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop` is measurable).

2. To show that `(G, H) ↦ G ⊓ H` is measurable, it suffices to show that for any measurable set `S` in `SimpleGraph V`, the preimage of `S` under `(G, H) ↦ G ⊓ H` is measurable in `SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V`. 

But we can instead use the fact that the σ-algebra on `SimpleGraph V` is generated by the sets `{G | G.adj u v}` for `u v : V`. 

Indeed, in Lean, `measurableSpace(SimpleGraph V)` is the pullback of `measurableSpace(V → V → Prop)`, and the latter is generated by the evaluations at `(u, v)`.

is measurable
      refine' h₂.prod h₃ |>.measurable
    exact h₁

by
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable G P := by
      rw [hG.hasLaw]
      exact measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isMeasurableEmbedding
        .aemeasurable (comap_ae (measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.measurableFun))
    have h₂ : AEMeasurable H P := by
      rw [hY.hasLaw]
      exact measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isMeasurableEmbedding
        .aemeasurable (comap_ae (measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.measurableFun))
    have h₃ : AEMeasurable (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the infimum operation is measurable
      have h₄ : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω ↦ G ω) P := h₁
      have h₅ : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω ↦ H ω) P := h₂
      -- The infimum of two measurable functions is measurable
      have h₆ : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) P := by
        -- Use the fact that the infimum operation is measurable
        have h₇ : Measurable (fun ω : Ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω : Ω → SimpleGraph V) := by
          -- The infimum of two measurable functions is measurable
          refine ‹_›
          _ : Measurable G := by
            -- G is measurable because it has the law G(V, p)
            have h₈ : HasLaw G G(V, p) P := by simpa using hG
            exact h₈.measurable
          _ : Measurable H := by
            -- H is measurable because it has the law G(V, q)
            have h₈ : HasLaw H G(V, q) P := by simpa using hY
            exact h₈.measurable
          _ : Measurable (inf : SimpleGraph V → SimpleGraph V → SimpleGraph V) := by
            -- The infimum operation is measurable
            refine measurable_of_forall_adj
            intro u v
            simp [SimpleGraph.Adj.inf]
            <;> (try aesop) <;> (try norm_num) <;>
              (try simp_all [measurable_iff_adj]) <;>
              (try simp_all [measurable_pi_iff]) <;>
              (try simp_all [measurable_const]) <;>
              (try simp_all [measurable_id]) <;>
              (try simp_all [measurable_inf]) <;>
              (try simp_all [measurable_congr])
            <;> simp_all [measurable_const, measurable_id]
            <;> try { aesop }
            <;> try { norm_num }
          <;> try simp_all [measurable_const, measurable_id]
          <;> try { aesop }
          <;> try { norm_num }
        -- Use the fact that the infimum operation is measurable
        exact h₄.and_measurable h₇
      -- The infimum of two measurable functions is measurable
      exact h₆
    -- The infimum of two measurable functions is measurable
    exact h₃

by
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P := by
      -- Since `G` and `H` are binomial random graphs, each is AEMeasurable.
      -- The infimum of two AEMeasurable functions is AEMeasurable.
      have h₂ : AEMeasurable G P := hG.aemeasurable -- Assume `hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P` includes `aemeasurable`.
      have h₃ : AEMeasurable H P := hY.aemeasurable -- Assume `hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P` includes `aemeasurable`.
      -- The infimum of two adjacent matrices is AEMeasurable because `⊓` is a measurable operation.
      have h₄ : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P := by
        refine' h₂.inf h₃
      exact h₄
    exact h₁

by
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable G P := hG.h
    have h₂ : AEMeasurable H P := hY.h
    have h₃ : AEMeasurable (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) P := by
      appliesh AM.eproduct.measurable_prod_mk.comp
      <;>
      (try simp_all [IsBinomialRandom]) <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₄ : AEMeasurable G P := hG.h
          have h₅ : AEMeasurable H P := hY.h
          map h₄.inf h₅
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₆ : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => (G ω, H ω)) P := AEMeasurable.prod_mk hG.h hY.h
          have h₇ : Measurable (fun p : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => p.1 ⊓ p.2) := by
            infer_instance
          exact h₆.comp h₇
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₈ : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => (G ω, H ω)) P := AEMeasurable.prod_mk hG.h hY.h
          have h₉ : Measurable (fun p : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => p.1 ⊓ p.2) := by
            infer_instance
          exact h₈.comp h₉
        })
    exact h₃
  law_eq := by sorry

by
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω) P := hG.aemeasurable
    have h₂ : AEMeasurable (fun ω => H ω) P := hY.aemeasurable
    have h₃ : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P :=
      h₁.mk (fun ω => H ω) h₂ (fun ω => _) |>.ne
      -- We need to show that the intersection operation is measurable.
      -- This follows from the fact that the intersection of two measurable sets is measurable.
      -- However, since we are working with random graphs, we need to use the fact that the intersection
      -- of two binomial random graphs is also a binomial random graph with parameter p * q.
      -- This is a more involved step and requires further justification.
      -- For now, we assume that the intersection operation is measurable.
      -- In a full proof, we would provide the necessary justification here.
      <;> simp_all [hG, hY]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₃

by
    have hG_m : Measurable G := (hG.hasLaw).measurable
    have hH_m : Measurable H := (hY.hasLaw).measurable
    have h_inf_m : Measurable (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) := by
      apply Measurable.inf hG_m hH_m
    exact h_inf_m.aemeasurable
